extracted_information: The web content provides a comprehensive guide to age verification compliance for selling age-restricted products in-store and online, detailing legal requirements, financial consequences of non-compliance, outdated and modern implementation methods, and checkout integration strategies. It highlights the importance of robust solutions to prevent sales to minors and protect businesses from fines and legal issues.

modern_verification_methods_in_store: Device-based age verification (liveness detection, fraud-resistant ID scans)

ftx_identity_key_features.seamless_integration: POS & Ecommerce (FTx Commerce, WooCommerce, Shopify, BigCommerce)
multi_layered_id_validation: Near-Field Communication (NFC) for contactless ID reads
ftx_identity_key_features.remote_on_demand_verification: Supports curbside pickup, delivery, mobile ordering
ftx_identity_key_features.protection_against_failure: Uses AI to analyze documents, verify biometrics, and ensure valid IDs

id_verification_provider_selection_crite: Compliance with all federal and state laws
Handles both in-store and online sales
Integrates easily with POS or ecommerce platform
Easy for customers to use (quick and simple)
Offers combination of document scanning, biometrics, and device checks (multiple layers of security)
Supports automatic reverification for repeat buyers
red_flags_in_providers: Poor data storage practices (unclear retention, insecure transmission)
compliance_checklist: Train staff thoroughly on ID scanning and fraud signals
Set up POS-integrated scanners that automatically prompt for verification and log events
Enable ecommerce plugins for frictionless age verification at checkout (mobile/desktop)
Maintain detailed audit logs and review regularly
Schedule periodic reverification for repeat/subscription customers

pricing.ftx_identity_advanced_options: Additional options for hands-free verification, EAIV integration, multi-location setups; pricing upon request.

features: Age restrictions to prevent misuse by minors and protect public health/safety
Compliance with federal and state laws (e.g., National Minimum Drinking Age Act, Tobacco 21 law)
Automated customer reverification for ongoing protection
Build trust with consumers by demonstrating compliance and safety
Unlock targeted marketing for verified customers
Reduce chargebacks, false declines, and payment disputes
Upsell memberships/subscription services to verified users
Streamline re-engagement with legally cleared customer base

statistics.tobacco_vape_id_check_failure: Nearly half (49.8%) of tobacco and vape shops didn’t check IDs when underage decoys attempted to buy vape products (JAMA Network study)
statistics.fake_id_flagging_rate: Between 2–5% of all scanned IDs are flagged as fake (IDScan.net)

age_restricted_products_by_category.cannabis_cbd: Varies by state (21+ for recreational in legalized states; medical under 21 with physician approval; CBD often 18+ or 21+)
age_restricted_products_by_category.adult_content_items: 18+ or 21+ (Varies by state and item type; federal law prohibits under 18)
age_restricted_products_by_category.lottery_tickets_gambling: 21+ in some states (e.g., AZ, IA, LA) and for most casino gambling; online typically 21+
age_restricted_products_by_category.over_the_counter_medications: 18+ for select products (e.g., pseudoephedrine); otherwise no strict age limit

new_york_alcohol_tobacco.alcohol_fines: $2,500-$4,000 per violation; up to $10,000 for severe/repeat offenses (NYSLA)
new_york_alcohol_tobacco.license_risk: Yes — suspension or revocation after repeat offenses

california_cannabis.selling_to_minors_fine: Classified as a felony (Health & Safety Code §11361) → 3-7 years in state prison
california_cannabis.license_risk: Yes — felony conviction can permanently void licenses

texas_tobacco_vape.fines: No universal statewide fine structure, local jurisdictions commonly impose $300-$2,500+
texas_tobacco_vape.license_risk: Yes — local and state permits can be suspended or revoked

florida_alcohol.fines: $1,000-$5,000 per incident (Florida DBPR guidelines); aggravated or repeat offenses can prompt license suspension
florida_alcohol.license_risk: Yes — license suspension likely, especially after second offense

nationwide_ecommerce.fines: Civil penalties up to $50,120 per violation (FTC & COPPA); additional liability if minor harmed or deceptive marketing
nationwide_ecommerce.license_risk: Yes — platforms and merchants can face bans, injunctions, and major lawsuits
